articles.full_text:
A dust storm hit parts of Delhi-NCR on Tuesday as the IMD issued an orange alert for rain, thunderstorms and strong winds. Weather officials forecast moderate to heavy rainfall and gusty winds across the region before conditions turn dry later in the day.
June 23, 2026 / 15:10 IST
Dust storm hit Delhi-NCR (Photo/ANI) - Dust storm hits Delhi-NCR, IMD issues orange alert for rain
- Moderate to heavy rain and strong winds expected in the region
- Weather to turn dry again later in the evening
Did our AI summary help?
A dust storm swept across parts of Delhi-NCR on Tuesday afternoon, reducing visibility and bringing temporary relief from the prevailing heat as the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued an orange alert for rain, thunderstorms and strong winds across the region. Forecasts indicated that dust storm conditions affecting parts of Gurugram and Delhi were likely to spread to other areas of the National Capital Region within a short span, HT reported.
The IMD predicted a brief spell of moderate to heavy rain accompanied by thunderstorms between 3 pm and 4:30 pm, with weather conditions expected to turn dry again later in the evening. Residents were advised to remain cautious due to the possibility of gusty winds and sudden weather changes.
According to the IMD's latest forecast for Delhi, the city is expected to witness a partly cloudy sky with the possibility of very light to light rain, thunderstorms, lightning and strong surface winds of 30-40 kmph gusting up to 50 kmph towards the afternoon and evening on June 23. Maximum temperatures are likely to remain between 39°C and 41°C.
